title: Smoky Spicy Malaysian Chicken Curry
description: A velvety, smoky curry that balances heat with subtle sweetness, perfect for a crowd-pleasing low-sodium meal.
instructions: Heat oil in a large skillet or wok over medium‑high heat. Add sliced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3–4 minutes.
Add minced garlic, grated ginger, and sliced chilies; stir‑fry for 1 minute until fragrant.
Sprinkle in the curry powder and smoked paprika, cooking for another 30 seconds to toast the spices.
Toss in the cubed chicken, browning all sides for 5 minutes to develop a slight smoky aroma.
Pour in the coconut milk and low‑sodium chicken broth, bringing the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Stir in fish sauce and brown sugar, then reduce heat to medium‑low; let the curry cook for 15–18 minutes until the chicken is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Taste and adjust seasoning—add more fish sauce for saltiness or more sugar for balance, keeping sodium low.
Remove from heat and let rest for 5 minutes so flavors meld. Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve with steamed jasmine rice or flatbread.
Optional: for an extra smoky touch, briefly toast a pinch of cumin seeds in a dry pan before adding to the curry.
